Both are directly interested in
securing Spokane franchises, and
the Washington Water Power CON-
pany especially appears to have
strong reasons for fighting the sub
mission of those franchises to popu-
lar vote in case the public cares
to pass on them.


Referendum advocates argue that
the principal use of the plan they
advocate Is to remove municipal
government from the hands OF
"tenderloin bosses" and cooperative
influences. They point significant
ly to the opposition they have
aroused in this city in support Of
this claim.


Else why, they asw, is Dutch
Jake" bringing all the influence OF
Occur d'Alene headquarters to bear
against the referendum issue in
conjunction with corporation
agents. They must fear the loss
of power and prestige, it is argued,
if the referendum carries.


And the supporters Of that meas
lie are asking the voters generally
if they deseve help or hindrance
in a fight Of this sort. They are
offering their proposed reform on
the strength of the enemies 1t
makes in addition to the benefits
they claim it will confer in the line
of better government for the good
of the people as opposed to the
forces that constantly demand
"protection" and the monopolistic
organization that make politics a
part of their business policy for
business" reasons.
